perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Restrictions on Sales of Securities . Each party is aware, and will advise its Representatives who are informed of the matters that are the subject of this Agreement, of the restrictions imposed by the United States securities laws on the purchase or sale of securities by any person who has received material, non-public information from the issuer of such securities and on the communication of such information to any other person when it is reasonably foreseeable that such other person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance upon such information. However, this will not prevent Receiving Party from purchasing stock through its benefit plans in the ordinary course of business.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1 Entire Agreement . This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties concerning the confidentiality of the Proprietary Information in connection with the Proposed Transaction and related matters and supersedes all prior or contemporaneous representations, discussions. Proposals, negotiations, conditions, communications and agreements, whether oral or written, between the parties relating to the same and all past courses of dealing or ndustry custom. Each party is aware, and will advise its Representatives who are informed of the matters that are the subject of this Agreement, of the restrictions imposed by the United States securities laws on the purchase or sale of securities by any person who has received material, non-public information from the issuer of such securities and on the communication of such information to any other person when it is reasonably foreseeable that such other person is likely to purchase or sell such securities in reliance upon such information. However, this will not prevent Receiving Party from purchasing stock through its benefit plans in the ordinary course of business.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the section regarding securities restrictions to 'Entire Agreement' obscures its importance and may lead parties to overlook it, reducing its enforceability. Securities restrictions are typically found in a separate section to emphasize the gravity of violations.
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 10b-5 requires companies to make sure that employees and related parties follow specific procedures to prevent insider trading. Placing restrictions on sales of securities into the 'Entire Agreement' section may not sufficiently highlight these procedures and risks leading to violations.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. CFR prev | next § 240.10b-5 Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. It shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly, by the use of any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce, or of the mails or of any facility of any national securities exchange , (a) To employ any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud, (b) To make any untrue statement of a material fact or to omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements made, in the light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, or (c) To engage in any act, practice, or course of business which operates or would operate as a fraud or deceit upon any person, in connection with the purchase or sale of any security . (Sec. 10; 48 Stat. 891 ; 15 U.S.C. 78j ) [ 13 FR 8183 , Dec. 22, 1948, as amended at 16 FR 7928 , Aug. 11, 1951] CFR Toolbox Law about... Articles from Wex Table of Popular Names Parallel Table of Authorities
perturbation.justification: The change moves the securities restrictions clause to the 'Entire Agreement' section, which may obscure its importance and decrease its enforceability. This is a structural flaw because it buries a legally required disclosure in an irrelevant section. Rule 10b-5, as described in the Law Explanation, requires companies to ensure employees follow procedures to prevent insider trading. By placing the restrictions in a less prominent section, the contract may not adequately highlight these procedures, increasing the risk of violations. Scraped Law Snippet 1 details that it is unlawful to employ any device to defraud in connection with the purchase or sale of any security, and obscuring the securities restrictions could be seen as a device or scheme to defraud. Thus, the structural flaw creates a significant risk of non-compliance.

perturbation.original_text: 9.5 Jurisdiction; Waiver of Jury Trial . The parties hereto agree that any and all actions or proceedings seeking to enforce any provision of, or based on any right arising out of, this Agreement shall be brought in the courts of the State of California, County of Los Angeles, including Federal Courts located therein, should Federal jurisdiction requirements exist. Each of the parties consents to the jurisdiction of such courts (and of the appropriate appellate courts) in any such action or proceeding and waives any objection to venue laid therein. Process in any action or proceeding referred to in the preceding sentence may be served on any party anywhere in the world. The parties hereto specifically waive any right to a jury trial with respect to any matter arising under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.  This Amendment shall be effective for all purposes as of the Amendment Date. The terms and conditions set forth in this Amendment are in addition to the terms of the Non-Disclosure Agreement which, except as amended in this Amendment, shall continue to remain in full force and effect in accordance with its terms. Any capitalized terms not otherwise defined herein shall have the meanings given such terms in the Non-Disclosure Agreement. The parties hereto agree that any and all actions or proceedings seeking to enforce any provision of, or based on any right arising out of, this Agreement shall be brought in the courts of the State of California, County of Los Angeles, including Federal Courts located therein, should Federal jurisdiction requirements exist. Each of the parties consents to the jurisdiction of such courts (and of the appropriate appellate courts) in any such action or proceeding and waives any objection to venue laid therein. Process in any action or proceeding referred to in the preceding sentence may be served on any party anywhere in the world. The parties hereto specifically waive any right to a jury trial with respect to any matter arising under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the jurisdiction and jury trial waiver clause to the Amendment section obscures the fact that it is applying to the entire agreement, not just the amendment. This could cause confusion about whether the jurisdiction and waiver only apply to changes from the original agreement.
perturbation.law_explanation: The 7th Amendment guarantees the right to a jury trial. While this right can be waived, the waiver must be knowing and voluntary. Placing the jury trial waiver in an amendment, and not clearly indicating its applicability to the entire agreement, may lead a party to argue that the waiver was not fully understood to apply beyond the amendment itself, and therefore not a valid waiver under the 7th Amendment.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Seventh Amendment | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Seventh Amendment The Seventh Amendment continues a practice from English common law of distinguishing civil claims which must be tried before a jury (absent waiver by the parties) from claims and issues that may be heard by a judge alone. It only governs federal civil courts and has no application to civil courts set up by the states when those courts are hearing only disputes of state law . Learn more... Primary tabs Amendment VII In suits at common law, where the value in controversy shall exceed twenty dollars, the right of trial by jury shall be preserved, and no fact tried by a jury, shall be otherwise reexamined in any court of the United States, than according to the rules of the common law. Wex Resources Criminal Law Criminal Procedure Jury Other Resources US Code: Title 18 Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure ‹ Sixth Amendment up Eighth Amendment › U.S. Constitution Toolbox Explanation of the Constitution - from the Congressional Research Service
perturbation.justification: The changed text moves the jury trial waiver clause to the Amendment section, creating a structural flaw that could lead to ambiguity about whether the waiver applies only to the amendment or to the entire agreement. The Law Explanation suggests this ambiguity could undermine the knowing and voluntary nature of the waiver, potentially violating the 7th Amendment. Scraped Law Snippet 2 states that the Seventh Amendment preserves the right to trial by jury unless waived by the parties. While a waiver is possible, the amendment's location and lack of explicit application to the entire agreement weakens the waiver, making it potentially unenforceable and thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 No Solicitation of Employees . Interested Party agrees that neither it nor its affiliates will at any time until the one year anniversary of the Effective Date, directly or indirectly, employ or solicit for employment (i) any key technical or management personnel of the Company that has first been introduced by the Company to the Interested Party in connection with the Proposed Transaction or who was otherwise substantively involved in the discussions of the Proposed Transaction or (ii) any other person who is now employed as an officer of the other party or any of its affiliates; provided, that the foregoing restrictions hall not be deemed to prohibit Interested Party or its Representatives from making general public solicitations for employment for any position or from employing any employee of the Company who either responds to such a general solicitation for employment or otherwise contacts Interested Party on his or her own initiative and without solicitation by Interested Party in contravention of the above restriction. Additionally, the above restriction shall not be deemed to prohibit Interested Party from making offers of employment or offering retention packages to any employees of Company or any of its affiliates in connection with the Proposed Transaction, provided that such offers have been disclosed to the Board of Directors of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 “Representatives” means as to any person, its directors, officers, employees, agents and advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, financing sources, attorneys, accountants and their respective Representatives). Interested Party agrees that neither it nor its affiliates will at any time until the one year anniversary of the Effective Date, directly or indirectly, employ or solicit for employment (i) any key technical or management personnel of the Company that has first been introduced by the Company to the Interested Party in connection with the Proposed Transaction or who was otherwise substantively involved in the discussions of the Proposed Transaction or (ii) any other person who is now employed as an officer of the other party or any of its affiliates; provided, that the foregoing restrictions hall not be deemed to prohibit Interested Party or its Representatives from making general public solicitations for employment for any position or from employing any employee of the Company who either responds to such a general solicitation for employment or otherwise contacts Interested Party on his or her own initiative and without solicitation by Interested Party in contravention of the above restriction. Additionally, the above restriction shall not be deemed to prohibit Interested Party from making offers of employment or offering retention packages to any employees of Company or any of its affiliates in connection with the Proposed Transaction, provided that such offers have been disclosed to the Board of Directors of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the no-solicitation clause into the definition of "Representatives" diminishes its prominence, which can weaken its enforceability. Typically, non-solicitation clauses are separate and well-defined due to their significant implications.
perturbation.contradicted_law: California Business and Professions Code Section 16600
perturbation.law_explanation: California Business and Professions Code Section 16600 generally prohibits non-compete agreements. While narrowly tailored non-solicitation agreements may be enforceable, their enforceability depends on clear and unambiguous language. Placing a non-solicitation clause within a definition section might be interpreted as ambiguous or less prominent, making it more susceptible to challenge under Section 16600.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, BPC 16600. skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Business and Professions Code - BPC DIVISION 7. GENERAL BUSINESS REGULATIONS [16000 - 18107] ( Division 7 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 61. ) PART 2. PRESERVATION AND REGULATION OF COMPETITION [16600 - 17365] ( Part 2 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) CHAPTER 1. Contracts in Restraint of Trade [16600 - 16607] ( Chapter 1 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) 16600. (a) Except as provided in this chapter, every contract by which anyone is restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business of any kind is to that extent void. (b) (1) This section shall be read broadly, in accordance with Edwards v. Arthur Andersen LLP (2008) 44 Cal.4th 937, to void the application of any noncompete agreement in an employment context, or any noncompete clause in an employment contract, no matter how narrowly tailored, that does not satisfy an exception in this chapter. (2) This subdivision does not constitute a change in, but is declaratory of, existing law. (c) This section shall not be limited to contracts where the person being restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business is a party to the contract. (Amended by Stats. 2023, Ch. 828, Sec. 1. (AB 1076) Effective January 1, 2024.)
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a structural flaw by embedding a no-solicitation clause within the definition of "Representatives," thereby diminishing its prominence and potentially weakening its enforceability. This contradicts the principle that non-compete or non-solicitation agreements must be clear and unambiguous to be enforceable, as highlighted by the Law Explanation. California Business and Professions Code Section 16600, as indicated in Scraped Law Snippet 1, generally voids contracts restraining someone from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business. While non-solicitation agreements can be narrowly tailored to be enforceable, placing such a critical clause within a definition section could be interpreted as an attempt to obscure or downplay its significance, thereby making it susceptible to challenge under Section 16600 because it is less 'clear and unambiguous'.
